description: Following the 2008 collapse of Iceland's three major commercial banks, the government faced pressure to repay foreign creditors (Icesave). A 2011 referendum allowed the public to vote on whether to accept the repayment terms; the public overwhelmingly voted against the bailout plan.

key_events.description: UK and Netherlands threaten legal action to force repayment.
President Grímsson announces he will veto the bill, triggering the referendum.
Final televised debates emphasize 'national sovereignty' vs 'economic stability'.
The referendum fails with nearly 60% of voters rejecting the Icesave deal.

calibration_notes: Highlights the rare dynamic where a nation chooses sovereign default over international bailouts, showing high volatility in public sentiment.
